description_long: This object is a compact, rectangular structure constructed primarily from red brick with a flat, concrete roof. The front facade features a narrow horizontal slit with metal bars, resembling a secure window or ventilation opening, and a recessed area behind the bars. The sides and rear are solid brick walls with no visible openings, emphasizing security and containment. The roof is slightly raised at the edges, forming a shallow parapet. The interior, visible through the barred opening, appears to be tiled and enclosed, reinforcing the impression of a secure holding area. The overall design is stark and utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on durability and security.
description: A small, brick-and-concrete prison structure with a barred front opening and solid, secure walls. Its utilitarian design emphasizes security and containment.
description_view_0: The front view shows a brick wall with a narrow, barred horizontal opening and a recessed interior space.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a solid brick wall with no openings or features.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a wide, open area with a large barred section and a visible tiled interior, covered by a concrete overhang.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing a plain brick wall without any openings.
